stories.title: State-of-the-art air defense systems have not been able to stop some Iranian missiles
stories.description: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.

stories.title: Sweden's top general says U.S. remains 'really important ally'
stories.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.

stories.title: Greetings from Turkey's border with Iran, where Iranians let loose on the dance floor
stories.description: Iranians escaping hardship and war are shaking it off to Persian, Arabic and Turkish tunes in this disco in eastern Turkey.

stories.title: Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war and lays out 5 conditions
stories.description: Iran says no to President Trump's ceasefire plan and publicized a counterproposal that includes war reparations and Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

stories.title: Iran's foreign minister says there are no negotiations with US
stories.description: Trump says he is talking to "the right people" in Iran, but Tehran says no such negotiations are being held.

stories.title: 'There's no safety anymore': Palestinians warn of expanding West Bank settler violence
stories.description: There has been a surge of attacks by Israeli settlers on Palestinian villages across the occupied territory since the start of the Iran war.

stories.title: Small window open for US-Iran talks, but swift end to war still unlikely
stories.description: President Trump says the US is already dealing with a "top person" in Iran, but Tehran denies that any talks have begun.

stories.title: What Iranians make of the possibility of talks to end the war
stories.description: Some Iranians dismiss the possibility of talks to end the war, others worry a deal may leave the current leaders in place.

stories.title: Iran war shows norms of international conflicts have been overturned
stories.description: US threats against energy infrastructure, and Iran's retaliation on its Gulf neighbours, signal a clear change.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

stories.title: Iranian missiles injure 180 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
stories.description: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: Why does the US have Iran's Kharg Island in its sights?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Live: Israel carries out fresh wave of strikes across Iran
stories.description: Israel's military said Thursday its forces had carried out a wave of strikes across Iran, as Tehran continues to insist that it will not negotiate on a peace deal. US President Donald Trump has warned he is ready to “unleash hell” if Iran does not accept a deal to end the nearly ...

stories.title: Ousted Venezuelan president Maduro to appear in New York court
stories.description: Ousted Venezuelan president Nicolas Maduro is set to appear in a New York court on Thursday for the second time since his dramatic capture by US forces in January. Maduro faces multiple charges including drug trafficking and weapons offences, as his lawyers challenge legal and pr...

stories.title: Lukashenko greeted by Kim Jong Un on first visit to North Korea
stories.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko made his first visit to North Korea on Wednesday, where he was warmly received by Kim Jong Un. The trip highlights growing ties between the two Moscow-aligned nations, both of which have supported Russia’s war in Ukraine, even as human ri...

stories.title: Oil, stock trading spikes before Trump's Iran remarks spark insider trading suspicions
stories.description: Thousands of oil contracts -- a far higher volume than normal -- were traded 15 minutes before Trump pledged to halt strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure, sending prices tumbling. Traders who bet on prices dropping ahead of the announcement would likely have profited from Tru...

stories.title: Iran rejects US peace plan, issues its own demands
stories.description: Iranian state television’s English-language broadcaster quoted an anonymous official as saying Iran rejected America’s ceasefire proposal and has its own demands for an end to the fighting. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran. He explains that the Iranian leadership is "be...

stories.title: Iran media casts doubt on US peace plan as strikes land across the Mideast
stories.description: State media in Iran said Wednesday that Tehran had rejected a US plan to end fighting, as the head of the United Nations warned that the Middle East war was spiralling out of control. In a first public acknowledgement of US efforts to discuss peace, state television cited an unid...

stories.title: Trump's war, NATO's problem: Will allies help secure Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: It’s Trump’s war, but is it also NATO’s problem? Europeans forced to bear security and financial consequences of the second war at its doorstep. We'll see if our panel agrees with Nato secretary-general Mark Rutte who assures it’s only a matter of “a couple of weeks” before tradi...

stories.title: Iran targets US public opinion with online information war
stories.description: The Iranian regime has been running an online influence campaign using AI-generated photographs, videos and memes to sow confusion about the war in the Middle East. Experts say the main goal is to ramp up anti-war sentiment in the US and pressure President Trump to end the confli...

stories.title: Governments move to shield consumers from soaring energy costs due to Mideast war
stories.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has rattled global markets, with oil prices up around 40 percent since the start of the US-Israeli war on Iran. Governments are now scrambling to shield ⁠consumers from soaring energy costs.

stories.title: Israel's defence network challenged by Iranian cluster ballistic missiles
stories.description: As the regional conflict between Israel and Iran enters a volatile new phase, a banned weapon of war is reappearing over civilian centres. While Israel’s defence systems remain robust, a new threat - the cluster ballistic missile - is testing the limits of safety. Unlike a single...

stories.title: Oil prices rise higher as Iran denies US talks, dimming deescalation hopes
stories.description: Brent crude tops $104 a barrel as hopes fade for deescalation in US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: Malaysia’s prime minister says Iran talks should end war
stories.description: Malaysia’s Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said any talks with Iran must aim to end the war.

stories.title: Russia slams second US-Israeli strike at Iran’s Bushehr nuclear reactor
stories.description: Russia’s foreign ministry has accused the US and Israel of trying to spark a nuclear disaster after a strike at Bushehr.

stories.title: Australia bans visitors from Iran amid war in the Middle East
stories.description: Australia's Home Affairs Department said decision to ban Iranian visitors amid the war was in the 'national interest'.

stories.title: Malaysia moves to tighten rules for expats, raising fears of talent flight
stories.description: Malaysia's government plans to slash the number of foreign workers to encourage local hiring and raise incomes.

stories.title: Harrison: ‘There is going to be some kind of a military escalation.’
stories.description: Ross Harrison says public talk of diplomacy between the US and Iran is hiding hardline positions.

stories.title: Tehran issues warning to regional neighbour if Iranian island occupied
stories.description: Iran's parliament speaker issues threat as US administration talks peace while committing more troops to conflict.

stories.title: Trump says Iranian leaders want deal ‘so badly’ but fear retaliation
stories.description: Donald Trump said Iran is negotiating with the US but is “afraid to say it”.

stories.title: Iran war live: US demands Tehran accept defeat; Israel pounds Lebanon
stories.description: Iranian official describes US's 15-point plan as 'maximalist' as state television outlines five conditions for peace.

stories.title: Egypt says it is ready to host Iran de-escalation talks
stories.description: Egypt’s Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty said Cairo is ready to host talks on Iran to support de-escalation.

stories.title: Iranian foreign minister rejects talks with US
stories.description: “No negotiations have taken place.” Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi says Tehran is not in talks with the US.

stories.title: Philippines farmers feeling the pinch of the war on Iran
stories.description: Cabbage farmers in the Philippines are harvesting their crops at a loss as prices plunge and fuel costs soar.

stories.title: Lebanon must not become next Gaza, says UN chief
stories.description: • Guterres warns war in Middle East is ‘out of control’; calls for immediate halt to hostilities between Israel, Hezbollah• Israeli strikes kill nine more in Beirut, southern areas• Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez condemns US-Israeli actions UNITED NATIONS: UN Secretary-General Antonio ...

stories.title: No progress as Iran, US stay entrenched, downplay prospects of direct talks

stories.title: Democrats win poll in Trump’s backyard
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Donald Trump, who has blasted mail-in ballots as “cheating”, cast his vote by mail in a Tuesday special election in Florida in which a Democrat won the district in the state legislature that includes the president’s Mar-a-Lago resort. Trump, who is pushing for nationa...

stories.title: Islamabad strengthens trade links with Tehran
stories.description: ISLAMABAD: In a move aimed at strengthening border trade management and improving documentation of cross-border flows, Pakistan has established a new customs station at Jeerak in Panjgur district, Balochistan, along the Pakistan-Iran border. The facility is expected to facilitate...

stories.title: Inte­r­national Organi­sation for Migration records surge in crossings from Iran to Pakistan
stories.description: ISLAMABAD: The Inte­r­national Organi­sation for Migration (IOM) has said in a report that a notable increase in crossings from Iran to Pakistan had emerged by mid-March. Titled ‘Regional Iran Escalation Mobility Mon­itoring’, the report stated that these crossings were driven by...

stories.title: Asia looks to pandemic playbook to combat fuel crisis
stories.description: SEOUL: Countries across Asia are weighing up work-from-home policies and stimulus measures enforced during the Covid pandemic as they scramble to respond to global fuel shortages triggered by the Iran war. Asia is at the frontline of the fuel crisis, buying more than 80 per cent ...

stories.title: Pakistan has conveyed US proposal; Turkiye or Pakistan could host talks, senior Iranian official says
stories.description: Pakistan delivered a proposal from the United States to Iran, and either Pakistan or Turkiye could be venues for discussions to de-escalate the war in the Gulf, a senior Iranian official told Reuters on Wednesday. The comments, by an official speaking on condition of anonymity, w...

stories.title: War-linked power crunch pushes Sri Lanka to 4-day week
stories.description: Millions of Sri Lankans enjoyed a government-ordered extra day off on Wednesday as the island nation battles an energy crisis triggered by the Middle East war. Rail and bus stations were largely deserted as most state institutions, schools and universities shifted to a four-day w...

stories.title: US reportedly sends peace plan as Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to ‘non-hostile’ oil vessels
stories.description: Washington sent a peace plan to Iran, US media reported, as Donald Trump voiced optimism Tuesday at ending nearly a month of warfare and Tehran announced that it will let “non-hostile” oil vessels go through the crucial Strait of Hormuz. The tentative signs of a diplomatic soluti...

stories.title: PM Shehbaz hails Saudi Arabia’s ‘restraint’, stresses urgent need for de-escalation of ongoing Iran war
stories.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Wednesday hailed Saudi Arabia for showing “remarkable restraint” during the ongoing Middle East hostilities and stressed the need for urgent de-escalation. He made the remarks during a phone call with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, wherei...

stories.title: Pakistan’s mediation offer reflects growing diplomatic confidence, Capitol Hill symposium told
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Speakers at an hours-long symposium on Capitol Hill on Tuesday noted that Pakistan’s gesture to mediate peace between the United States and Iran, amid the recent escalation of hostilities between Washington and Tehran, reflects growing confidence in Islamabad’s diplom...

stories.title: Pakistan joins UNSC in denouncing illegal Israeli settlements
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Pakistan on Tuesday joined seven other members of the UN Security Council in reiterating firm opposition to the annexation of any part of the Occupied Palestinian Territory and the forcible displacement of the Palestinian people. All seven nations warned that such act...

stories.title: AJK PM condemns sentencing of Kashmiri leader Asiya Andrabi, her associates by Indian court
stories.description: MUZAFFARABAD: Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) Prime Minister Raja Faisal Mumtaz Rathore on Tuesday strongly condemned the sentencing of three women activists by an Indian court, terming the verdict a manifestation of “political coercion” aimed at silencing dissent in India-occupied ...

stories.title: US-Israel war on Iran may increase food prices worldwide: UN
stories.description: ISLAMABAD: The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO) has said that the prolonged disruption to trade through the Strait of Hormuz triggered by US/Israeli attack on Iran could create cascading impacts across energy markets, fertiliser supply chains and global agri...

stories.title: Freak storms to hit war-ravaged Middle East
stories.description: A TORNADO Alley-style storm system is about to hit the Middle East — a virtually unheard of atmospheric setup for the region, the Washington Post reported, as areas in the Gulf region received heavy rains. Parts of the Arabian Peninsula and Persian Gulf could be slammed by strong...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
